raw_output_payload.reasoning:
Need a bash command to compare directories, list new/missing and differing files. Use diff -rq or rsync --dry-run. For summary sorted alphabetically. Maybe:

comm -3 <(find /system/folder1 -type f -printf "%P\n" | sort) <(find /system/folder2 -type f -printf "%P\n" | sort)

But need differences. Use diff -rq. Then parse. Simpler: diff -rq folder1 folder2 | sort. That prints differences. So final command: diff -rq /system/folder1 /system/folder2 | sort.
raw_output_payload.reasoning_content:
Need a bash command to compare directories, list new/missing and differing files. Use diff -rq or rsync --dry-run. For summary sorted alphabetically. Maybe:

comm -3 <(find /system/folder1 -type f -printf "%P\n" | sort) <(find /system/folder2 -type f -printf "%P\n" | sort)

But need differences. Use diff -rq. Then parse. Simpler: diff -rq folder1 folder2 | sort. That prints differences. So final command: diff -rq /system/folder1 /system/folder2 | sort.
